tlos: TLO 1. The student will complete AFI-required ground training and introductory ground training applicable to all TPS flying.

events.description: These classes are AFI-mandated training and are provided by 412th Operations Group trainers to meet AFI standards.

An introduction to handling emergencies in the cockpit of an aircraft. Students will develop an appreciation for the complexities and additional burdens levied on aircrew during an emergency and understand how to properly assist during one.

tlos: TLO 1. The student will understand how to correctly operate the DAS in all curriculum aircraft.

events.description: Flight records briefing and in-processing. Students are introduced to safety privilege as well as safety reporting and mishap requirements. Training on local area procedures.
Flight records briefing and in-processing. Students are introduced to safety privilege as well as safety reporting and mishap requirements. Training on local area procedures.

Issue and training for electronic flight bags (EFB). Time allocated at the end of the TPS to turn in individually issued EFBs.

Mandatory testing for aircraft operators.
Classroom and planeside instruction on the operation of the Data Acquisition Systems (DAS) installed in each curriculum aircraft.

tlos: TLO 1. The student will recall the concepts associated with crew resource management.

events.description: This block is required by AFI and prepares all students for safe and effective operation as a flight crew or test team, including in the control room.

tlos: TLO 1. Describe the airmanship & mission planning fundamentals required for safe, disciplined, and effective mission participation.
TLO 2. Demonstrate fundamental aircraft handling & systems management proficiency.
TLO 4. Develop a structured planning and briefing methodology.

events.description: The airmanship lecture introduces and reinforces basic aircraft concepts to students with limited aviation experience. The purpose is to build basic airmanship knowledge and skills required for students at TPS.

The airmanship lecture introduces and reinforces basic aircraft concepts to students with limited aviation experience. The purpose is to build basic airmanship knowledge and skills required for students at TPS.

The profiles for the flights are structured to start with the most basic aviation skills and subsequently build in complexity. The purpose of these hours is to expose the student to various aviation concepts common to ordinary flight. There is no requirement for these students to demonstrate solo proficiency in any maneuver.

Due to their previous aviation experience, CSO students will only receive 1-2 airmanship flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-14F.

This block covers a basic overview of the aircraft familiarization flights each ABM, CSO, FTE, and RPA student will have in the C-12, T-38, and F-16.

tlos: TLO 1. Recall key elements of high L/D aircraft operation and energy management.

events.description: The purpose of the glider familiarization flights is to become familiar with glider (high L/D) flying while observing and practicing energy management. All students will receive at least two glider flights.

The purpose of the glider familiarization flights is to become familiar with glider (high L/D) flying while observing and practicing energy management. All students will receive at least two glider flights.

tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate proficiency in T-38 operations.

events.description: The T-38 ground school will include an introduction to the T-38 check flight phase and instruction on T-38 systems to include electrical abnormalities, avionics, engines, flight controls, and hydraulics. Training covers safety equipment and egress procedures using either a training device or aircraft. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with T-38 mission preparation, aircraft procedures, operations limits, and emergency procedures. Training occurs at the aircraft.

Training occurs at the aircraft.

A complement to the T-38 Ground School, the self-paced Computer Based Training is to be accomplished in the student's own time. Topics include a systems overview, electrical, controls and displays, fuel system, air conditioning and pressurization.
Training on the procedures and techniques necessary to reduce the probability of a compressor stall and flameout while operating the T-38.
This testing includes the standard testing required in the AFI 11-2ftv2.

The objective of the T-38 check flights is to familiarize the student with T-38 aircraft operating procedures, AFTC local operations procedures, and primary divert base procedures. TPS students that meet the requirements of these blocks will be certified "crew solo".

The objective of the T-38 check flights is to familiarize the student with T-38 aircraft operating procedures, AFTC local operations procedures, and primary divert base procedures. TPS students that meet the requirements of these blocks will be certified "crew solo".

The objective of the T-38 training flight is to introduce students to T-38 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well apply basic aerodynamic principles during their sortie.

modules.moduleName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Training
tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate proficiency in Learjet operations.

events.eventName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Ground School
events.description: Introduces Calspan's Variable Stability System Learjet. The ground school will cover basic operating procedures, limits, and safety/egress. The objective of the Learjet check flights is to provide working knowledge of the Learjet Ground Control Station. This training is for students in the Learjet data group and will consist of two flights operated from the ground control station.

Introduces Calspan's Variable Stability System Learjet. The ground school will cover basic operating procedures, limits, and safety/egress. The objective of the Learjet check flights is to provide working knowledge of the Learjet Ground Control Station. This training is for students in the Learjet data group and will consist of two flights operated from the ground control station.

Introduces Calspan's Variable Stability System Learjet. The ground school will cover basic operating procedures, limits, and safety/egress. The objective of the Learjet check flights is to provide working knowledge of the Learjet Ground Control Station. This training is for students in the Learjet data group and will consist of two flights operated from the ground control station.
Each student will also fly one flight onboard the Calspan Learjet for orientation with the VSS system. This can be completed in conjunction with CF 6402-3F.

prerequisites.name: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Ground School

tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate proficiency in C-12 operations.

events.description: Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

The purpose of the C-12 check flights is to familiarize the student with C-12 aircraft operating procedures, local orientation of R-2515, and an orientation of the Edwards and Palmdale patterns. These blocks include three sorties for pilots only in the C-12 data group. TPS students that complete the blocks will be certified "crew solo" in the C-12.

These blocks include three sorties for pilots only in the C-12 data group. TPS students that complete the blocks will be certified "crew solo" in the C-12.

These blocks include three sorties for pilots only in the C-12 data group. TPS students that complete the blocks will be certified "crew solo" in the C-12.

The objective of the C-12 training flights is to introduce students to C-12 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well as learn to calculate basic aircraft TOLD.

tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate proficiency in F-16 operations.
TLO 2. Demonstrate proficiency in control room operations.

events.description: Classroom instruction introducing the F-16 check flight training requirements, a systems overview, and a detailed look at normal and emergency procedures. The academics also provide control room interaction training requirements, control room role in systems monitoring, and how the control room interacts during emergency procedures. Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.

Classroom instruction introducing the F-16 check flight training requirements, a systems overview, and a detailed look at normal and emergency procedures. The academics also provide control room interaction training requirements, control room role in systems monitoring, and how the control room interacts during emergency procedures. Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.

Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.

Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.

Classroom instruction introducing the F-16 check flight training requirements, a systems overview, and a detailed look at normal and emergency procedures. The academics also provide control room interaction training requirements, control room role in systems monitoring, and how the control room interacts during emergency procedures. Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.
This simulator is executed by pilot students to practice emergency procedures but will be observed by all other students for awareness.

The asynchronous block introduces students to basic control room operations and presents the IADS analysis software to the students. In addition to providing principles for good control room screen design, the block introduces the major capabilities of the IADS software as well as techniques to confirm proper system operation prior to mission start. Students will learn how to create real-time analysis windows in IADS, how to display data effectively, how to insert event markers and use test points to take notes, and how to use inherent IADS capabilities to streamline real-time execution.

The objective of the Control Room Familiarization is to introduce the student to control room operations in a mission playback environment. The control room observation can be conducted along with any other curriculum control room event using live aircraft.
The control room observation can be conducted along with any other curriculum control room event using live aircraft.
Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 closed book testing.

This simulator ensures the student can adequately respond to emergency procedures during crew solo operations prior to their final CF flight.

The purpose of the F-16 check flights is to familiarize the student with F-16 aircraft operating procedures, handling qualities, maneuvers, simulated flameout patterns, and operational limitations. Students will also have a working knowledge of the local complex and airspace. The blocks include four sorties for F-16 data group pilots only. TPS students that meet the requirements on all four sorties will be certified "crew solo" in the F-16.

The purpose of the F-16 check flights is to familiarize the student with F-16 aircraft operating procedures, handling qualities, maneuvers, simulated flameout patterns, and operational limitations. Students will also have a working knowledge of the local complex and airspace. The blocks include four sorties for F-16 data group pilots only. TPS students that meet the requirements on all four sorties will be certified "crew solo" in the F-16.

The purpose of the F-16 check flights is to familiarize the student with F-16 aircraft operating procedures, handling qualities, maneuvers, simulated flameout patterns, and operational limitations. Students will also have a working knowledge of the local complex and airspace. The blocks include four sorties for F-16 data group pilots only. TPS students that meet the requirements on all four sorties will be certified "crew solo" in the F-16.

The purpose of the F-16 check flights is to familiarize the student with F-16 aircraft operating procedures, handling qualities, maneuvers, simulated flameout patterns, and operational limitations. Students will also have a working knowledge of the local complex and airspace. The blocks include four sorties for F-16 data group pilots only. TPS students that meet the requirements on all four sorties will be certified "crew solo" in the F-16.

The objective of the F-16 training flight is to introduce non-pilot students to F-16 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well as observe basic aerodynamic principles during their sortie. Non-data group pilots will receive the F-16 Training Flight to introduce F-16 procedures, systems operations, and checklist compliance. The purpose of the training flight for student pilots, not in the F-16 group, is to provide a limited scope orientation flight to help prepare them for future flights on one of the main curriculum platforms.

Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well as observe basic aerodynamic principles during their sortie. Non-data group pilots will receive the F-16 Training Flight to introduce F-16 procedures, systems operations, and checklist compliance. tlos: TLO 1. Demonstrate proficiency in formation flight.

events.description: These events orient the students with formation flying and prepare them for curriculum events required chase and target support.

tlos: TLO 1: Adapt to piloting high performance aircraft.
TLO 2: Demonstrate partial proficiency in C-12 & T-38 operations.

events.description: Due to their previous aviation experience, RPA pilot students will only receive 1-2 C-172 flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-4F. Emphasis will be placed on improving manned aircraft piloting skills.

Due to their previous aviation experience, RPA pilot students will only receive 1-2 C-172 flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-4F. Emphasis will be placed on improving manned aircraft piloting skills.

Single ship C-12 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after the C-172 flight(s). Also, this flight introduces to the student C-12 flight operations.

Single ship T-38 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after the CF-3 C-12 Training Flight. Also, this flight introduces the student to T-38 flight operations.
Formation C-12 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after CF-3 or CF-5.
Formation T-38 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after CF-4 and may be accomplished prior to CF-5.
